title: The Control Kill List
subtitle: We mutation-tested our own controls. For every control we injected the exact defect it exists to catch. A control that does not fire on its own named defect is theatre.

headline.note: Cumulative across two passes. Pass 1 covered the highest-tier customer-impacting set (13). Pass 2 extended to the inventoried-but-untested tail (14 more): the compliance trackers, the health / page-consistency gates, the daemon change-detection gate, plus a structural read-only check of the LLM-judge evaluators (their judgement quality is documented as not deterministically mutation-testable, not counted here). The 1 THEATRE control remains the flagship vat_by_segment arithmetic check -- a tautology, mitigated by an independent cross-check that fires. Pass 2 found 5 new killer-pattern GAPS (KL-4..KL-8) registered for the orchestrator; every pass-2 control still fires on its core named defect, so pass-2 theatre count is 0.

examples: social_obligation is_compliant status-trust (KL-6) -- mitigated by independent underspend check
pre-bill gate skipped VAT validation on subtotal <= 0 (FIXED)
Qwen internal-audit backstop returned clean when Ollama was down (FIXED)

controls.name: Resi consumption plausibility
controls.defect: SME-scale load on a resi record

controls.name: Population consumption vs TDCV
controls.defect: SME-scale annual load across a resi book

controls.name: Population unit rate vs Ofgem cap band
controls.defect: population drifting off real market pricing

controls.name: Consumer Duty RAG aggregator
controls.defect: a RED outcome not escalating the register

controls.name: Crisis bad-debt step-up diagnostic
controls.defect: flat trajectory with no 2021-22 crisis step-up
controls.note: R12 diagnostic; fails closed on missing crisis data

controls.name: Green claims REGO coverage audit
controls.defect: green claim not backed by held REGOs

controls.name: R14 basis-labels gate
controls.defect: headline GBP figure with no basis (clock) label

controls.name: Page population-consistency gate
controls.defect: Book Size not reconciling to source population

controls.name: Stack health check
controls.note: fails closed (alarms) when tmux+ps both unavailable

controls.name: Stale-running-code check (R2/R3)
controls.defect: daemon process older than its own script

controls.name: Run change-detection (dedup) gate
controls.defect: skipping a genuinely-changed run / spurious skip on corrupt memory
controls.note: fingerprint sensitive to change; fails closed on unreadable dedup memory

controls.name: LLM-judge evaluators (structural)
controls.defect: a judge able to Write/Edit its way to a PASS
controls.note: read-only guarantee asserted; verdict quality not deterministically mutation-testable

fixed_this_pass.before: returned clean when Ollama was down; audit passed by not running
fixed_this_pass.after: third 'unavailable' verdict alarms; sanity daemon reports the outage
negative subtotal and VAT-on-nothing fail closed (HELD)

newly_found_gaps.gap: empty meter_read_log reads clean though total absence of reads is the most-broken state
newly_found_gaps.disposition: listed (empty can be a legitimate first-run; sanity_daemon consumes it)

newly_found_gaps.gap: empty register (zero assessments) reports GREEN; FCA Consumer Duty treats un-assessed as a governance failure
newly_found_gaps.disposition: listed (overall_rag has 14+ callers treating GREEN as baseline)

newly_found_gaps.gap: compliance from the self-declared status field, not spend-vs-target; a mislabelled-PAID underspend passes
newly_found_gaps.disposition: listed, mitigated by the independent underspend_records() control

newly_found_gaps.gap: obligation==0 short-circuits to 100% COMPLIANT; broken green-product detection reads compliant
newly_found_gaps.disposition: listed (zero obligation legitimately means no claims made)

newly_found_gaps.gap: missing run_insights.json returns pass; a one-sided missing headline key is silently skipped
newly_found_gaps.disposition: listed (first-run insights may legitimately be absent)

coverage_honesty: Pass 2 reaches near-full apparatus coverage of the DETERMINISTIC controls: compliance trackers, health/page-consistency gates, and the daemon change-detection gate are now mutation-tested. Still honestly UNCOVERED: the LLM-judge evaluators' verdict QUALITY (only their read-only structure is asserted -- no fixed prompt->NEEDS_WORK oracle exists), the daemon SCHEDULING loops (cadence/cooldown/restart backoff), and the long tail of accumulator-style regulatory registers. See control_registry.json for the full list and the 5 registered gaps (KL-4..KL-8).
